public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as communal duty supervisor.
appearance: Kairn Steelbrood carries the marks of communal duty supervisor in industrial grit: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: In conversation, Kairn Steelbrood is quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock; they listen for leverage, social risk, and the sentence beneath the sentence.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of communal duty supervisor in and around Embervale Settlement, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Embervale Settlement while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to work shortfalls is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Kairn Steelbrood can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Kairn Steelbrood can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Kairn Steelbrood carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Embervale Settlement, this lands inside a public field already shaped by regional quartermasters, shift-boss families.

who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Kairn Steelbrood presents as Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a communal duty supervisor still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Communal duty supervisor, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Kairn Steelbrood refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Communal duty supervisor too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Kairn Steelbrood can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Kairn Steelbrood adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Kairn Steelbrood is Known locally through day-to-day work as communal duty supervisor. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Communal duty supervisor.
social_texture: Kairn Steelbrood speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Embervale Settlement, people read them as quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to production; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but work shortfalls becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Kairn touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Kairn Steelbrood when the party needs a socially grounded face for Embervale Settlement: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
